mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth reflects the overall expansion of the economy, directly boosting corporate earnings and equity valuations. This mechanism is persistently active as GDP expands, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Forward returns over 60-day periods are negative more than 40% of the time when GDPC1 is positive quarter-over-quarter, or the rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and lowers borrowing costs, creating a supportive environment for equity prices. The current rate of 0.33% provides a strong tailwind for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is negative when FEDFUNDS is below 1% in more than 50% of instances, or if the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate from its cycle low of 3.6% signals labor market weakening and impending economic slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate profits and equity valuations. An increase above 4.5% confirms the deterioration and triggers a negative outlook for forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE is 3.6%, near historical lows.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, the forward 20-day return is positive more than 60% of the time, or the average return following such triggers is higher than the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity. A decline in housing starts below a critical threshold (e.g., 1.7 million units annualized) signals a downturn in construction and related industries, presaging weaker economic growth and lower equity returns over the next quarter. The latest reading is 1793 thousand units.
failure_condition: If after HOUST falls below 1700, the forward 60-day return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is extremely low and inflation (CPI) is rising, real interest rates become negative, creating distortions in capital allocation and fueling asset bubbles that eventually burst. This combination signals impending monetary tightening and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If the presence of both conditions leads to positive forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the joint signal does not produce a statistically significant negative return compared to univariate conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Discrepancy between employment growth and industrial production growth can signal structural imbalances. If payroll employment continues to grow while industrial production contracts, it may indicate that the services sector is masking weakness in manufacturing, which historically precedes equity market declines.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151314k, INDPRO at 104.5853 in September 2007.
failure_condition: If the divergence condition yields positive 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the joint signal has no predictive power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: In a full-employment regime (UNRATE below 4%), additional declines in unemployment are no longer beneficial as they intensify wage inflation pressures and trigger earlier Fed tightening. Thus, a lower UNRATE becomes a negative signal for equity returns. Current UNRATE at 3.6% places us firmly in this regime.
failure_condition: If in the full employment regime, a lower UNRATE (i.e., month-over-month decline) leads to positive 20-day returns more often than not, or the average return following declines is higher than following increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound (below 0.5%), conventional monetary policy is exhausted. Further rate cuts are interpreted as a signal of severe economic distress and limited central bank firepower, which is negative for equity markets. The current rate of 0.33% is in this regime.
failure_condition: If in the ZLB regime, a further decrease in FEDFUNDS (i.e., a rate cut) leads to positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the level of FEDFUNDS is positively correlated with forward returns.
